WorkoutDescription: FULL REST: No exercise. Stretching 15min, foam roll quads/calves. Compression if available. Hydration 3L. Start reducing portions and alcohol.
RECOVERY SESSION: 30min walking OR 30min stationary bike Z1 (minimal resistance). HR under 138. Blood flow for recovery, not fitness. Should feel effortless.

Ultra Goal: Rebuild upper-body and core posture muscles needed for 14h with poles and pack.

WARM-UP (5 min): Arm circles x20, Band pull-aparts x15, Scapular push-ups x10, Cat-cow x10

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Row — 3x12 — Rest 60s
A2. Shoulder Press — 3x12 — Rest 60s
B1. Push-up — 3x15 — Rest 60s
B2. Face pulls (band) — 3x15 — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 45s | Side Plank 30s each side | Russian Twist 20 reps with 5kg

COOL-DOWN (10 min): Thoracic opener 60s, Lat stretch 45s each, Child's pose 60s, Pec stretch 45s each

Details: First run post-UTBA, flat terrain only
WorkoutDescription: EASY RUN: 30min on flat terrain only. HR strictly under 138. If legs feel heavy after 15min, walk the rest. Don't force. This is a test, not a workout.

Ultra Goal: Restore full-body mobility and activate core without leg loading while still in post-UTBA recovery.

WARM-UP (20 min): Bike or rower Z1 (HR < 138) continuous

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Hip circles — 3x10 each side — Rest 30s
A2. Walking Lunge with rotation — 3x10 each leg — Rest 30s
A3. Deep squat hold — 3x45s — Rest 30s
A4. Ankle dorsiflexion stretch — 3x45s each — Rest 30s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Dead Bug 10 each side | Bird dog 10 each side | Plank 40s

COOL-DOWN (10 min): Pigeon pose 60s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each, Thoracic twist 45s each
EASY RUN: 50min easy trail ~8km / 150m D+. HR under 148. Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, slow down. Check that legs respond correctly before ramping up next week.

FULL REST: No exercise. Morning weigh-in (log it). Plan next week's mountain sessions. Meal prep Sunday evening. Acquire chest HR strap (Polar H10, Garmin HRM-Pro, Coros HR) for threshold test next Thursday.

Ultra Goal: Re-pattern legs with light volume; single-leg balance; and eccentric control before true progressive loading.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Glute bridges x15, Bodyweight squats x15, Ankle circles x15 each

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Back Squat — 3x15 (light goblet) — Rest 75s
A2. Walking Lunge — 3x12 each leg — Rest 60s
B1. Box Step-up — 3x15 each leg (40cm) — Rest 60s
B2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x12 (or slow leg curl) — Rest 60s
C1. Calf Raise — 3x20 — Rest 45s
C2. Back Squat (eccentric) — 3x8 (4s descent, light) — Rest 75s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 45s | Russian Twist 20 reps | Dead Bug 12 each side

COOL-DOWN (8 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each, Hamstring stretch 45s each

HILL REPEATS: 10min easy warm-up on flat. Main set: 5x2min on 10-15% grade hill (steady Z3 effort, HR 153-168). Walk/jog down recovery (HR drops below 140 before next rep). 10min easy cool-down. Focus: short steps, active hiking posture, pole push if available.

Ultra Goal: Build vertical-specific engine on stairs while maintaining upper-body posture for poles.

WARM-UP (25 min): Progressive stairmaster — 5min Z1 (<138), 10min Z2 (138-153), 5min Z3 (153-168), 5min Z2

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Row — 3x12 — Rest 60s
A2. Shoulder Press — 3x12 — Rest 60s
B1. Push-up (decline) — 3x15 — Rest 60s
B2. Face pulls (band) — 3x15 — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 45s | Superman 30s | Side Plank 30s each side

COOL-DOWN (8 min): Lat stretch 45s each, Thoracic opener 60s, Pec stretch 45s each

Details: 10min WU + 30min at threshold (CHEST HRM REQUIRED) + 10min CD
WorkoutDescription: THRESHOLD TEST: Chest HR strap MANDATORY (Polar H10, Garmin HRM-Pro or similar). 10min progressive warm-up (walk → easy jog). Main set: 30min at 'comfortably hard' effort on FLAT terrain — you can speak short sentences but not hold a conversation. Record AVERAGE HR of last 20 minutes of main set = approximate lactate threshold. 10min cool-down. LOG THE VALUE — zones may be recalibrated. Current zones are +8bpm corrected from UTBA wrist watch data.

Ultra Goal: Build eccentric tolerance in quads; calves; hamstrings — the exact muscles that fail first on steep descents.

WARM-UP (8 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, Bodyweight squats x15

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Back Squat (eccentric 4s descent, 1s up) — 4x8 — Rest 90s
A2. Bulgarian Split Squat (rear foot on bench) — 3x10 each leg — Rest 75s
B1. Calf Raise (eccentric, up 2 feet, down 1, 4s) — 3x15 each — Rest 60s
B2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x12 (or slow leg curl) — Rest 75s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 50s | Dead Bug 12 each side | Side Plank 35s each side

COOL-DOWN (15 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each, Ankle mobility 60s each

LONG RUN: 2h45 ~22km / 800m D+. HR strictly Z2 (138-153). WALK all uphills steeper than 15%. Run flats and gentle downhills. Poles if terrain allows. Fueling: 1 C90+salt per hour. Practice race fueling strategy. Wear chest HR strap to validate zones from Thursday's test.

Details: Easy jog or bike spin + stretching
WorkoutDescription: RECOVERY SESSION: 20min very easy jog Z1 (HR < 138) OR 30min easy bike + 20min full stretching (hamstrings, quads, calves, hips, back). Goal is blood flow for recovery, not fitness. Should feel effortless.

Ultra Goal: First true progressive overload session — add weight to last week's movements to trigger hypertrophy and strength gains.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Glute bridges x15, Bodyweight squats x12, Walking Lunge x10 each leg

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Back Squat — 4x10 (+5kg vs week 2) — Rest 90s
A2. Walking Lunge (with dumbbells) — 3x12 each leg — Rest 75s
B1. Box Step-up (tall bench, loaded) — 4x15 each leg — Rest 75s
B2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x12 (or leg curl) — Rest 75s
C1. Back Squat (eccentric 4s) — 4x8 — Rest 90s
C2. Calf Raise — 3x20 (seated + standing mix) — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 50s | Russian Twist 15 reps with 8kg | Dead Bug 10 each side weighted

COOL-DOWN (8 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each

Details: 10min WU + 6x4min hill sustained w/ poles + 10min CD
WorkoutDescription: HILL REPEATS: 10min easy warm-up Z1 (<138). Main set: 6x4min sustained uphill effort Z3-Z4 (HR 153-178) WITH POLES (technique: plant-push-release rhythm synced with steps). Recovery: jog/walk down between reps. 10min cool-down. Hill must be >10% grade. Simulates Gypaète climbs.

Ultra Goal: Extend vertical engine duration to 35 minutes while reinforcing upper-body strength for pole work.

WARM-UP (35 min): Stairmaster — 10min Z1 (<138), 15min Z2 (138-153), 5min Z3 (153-168), 5min Z2

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Row (heavy) — 3x12 — Rest 75s
A2. Shoulder Press — 3x12 — Rest 75s
B1. Push-up — 3x20 — Rest 60s
B2. Face pulls (band) — 3x15 — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 30s + Superman 30s | Side Plank 40s each side | Russian Twist 20 reps

COOL-DOWN (8 min): Thoracic opener 60s, Lat stretch 45s each, Pec stretch 45s each

TEMPO SESSION: 10min warm-up Z1. Main set: 45min at tempo pace Z2-Z3 (HR 143-166) on rolling trail ~12km / 350m D+. Gypaète target race pace (~10-11min/km). 15min cool-down Z1. Objective: find your sustainable cruising pace for race day.

Ultra Goal: Deepen eccentric loading — the single best protection against quad shutdown on Gypaète's long descents.

WARM-UP (8 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, Bodyweight squats x15

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Back Squat (eccentric 5s descent) — 4x10 — Rest 90s
A2. Bulgarian Split Squat (deep, with dumbbells) — 3x12 each leg — Rest 90s
B1. Calf Raise (eccentric, 4s) — 4x15 each — Rest 60s
B2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x12 — Rest 75s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 50s | Dead Bug 12 each side | Side Plank 40s each side

COOL-DOWN (15 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each, Ankle mobility 60s each, Quad stretch 45s each

LONG RUN WITH VERTICAL: 3h45 ~26km / 1100m D+. Significant mountain day. WALK steep uphills, poles, run flats and downhills. Full fueling: C90+salt/hour + 1 solid bar every 2 hours. Never exceed 158 bpm on climbs. Practice effort management.

EASY RUN (BACK-TO-BACK): ~9km / 200m D+ on rolling terrain day after long run. HR strictly under 148. Very easy pace. Train the body to move on pre-fatigued legs — this is the km 50+ simulation for Gypaète.

Ultra Goal: Convert strength into sustained power — the fast-twitch-to-slow-twitch bridge for steep vertical repeats.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Glute bridges x15, Bodyweight squats x15, High knees x30s

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Back Squat (moderate) — 4x12 — Rest 75s
A2. Box Jump (40cm) — 4x10 — Rest 75s
B1. Box Step-up — 4x15 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Walking Lunge (jump lunges) — 4x12 each leg — Rest 60s
C1. Calf Raise — 3x20 — Rest 45s
C2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x15 (or leg curl) — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 45s | Russian Twist 20 reps | Dead Bug 12 each side

COOL-DOWN (10 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each

Details: Vertical KM effort — biggest hill available
WorkoutDescription: HILL REPEATS (VERTICAL KM): 10min warm-up. Find biggest available hill (500-700m D+ in one shot). Climb in sustained Z3-Z4 effort (HR 153-183, fast hike + poles). Time it. Descend in Z1 jog (practice technical descent). If hill <500m D+, do 2 reps.

Ultra Goal: Push lactate threshold on the rower while bulletproofing the core that holds pack + pole posture at hour 12.

WARM-UP (10 min): Rower Z1 building to Z2 progressively, arm circles and scapular retraction mixed in

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Row (threshold) — 5x4min Z3-Z4 (HR 153-183) — Rest 2min active Z1
A2. Shoulder Press — 3x10 — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x4, 60s rest between rounds):
Plank 60s | Russian Twist 20 reps with 10kg | Side Plank 40s each side | Dead Bug 15 each side

COOL-DOWN (10 min): Thoracic opener 60s, Lat stretch 45s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each

EASY RUN: ~8km with 300m D-. Seek technical descents (roots, rocks, grade >15%). Practice: quick foot placement, eyes 3m ahead, arms wide for balance. HR under 148 on climbs (walk if needed). Prepare quads for Gypaète's 5000m D- at 90+kg.

Ultra Goal: Peak strength session — last heavy load before taper. Lock in max force production for uphill power.

WARM-UP (10 min): Leg swings x20 each, Glute bridges x20, Bodyweight squats x15, Walking Lunge x10 each leg

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Back Squat (heavy 5x5) — 5x5 — Rest 3min
A2. Back Squat (eccentric 6s) — 4x8 — Rest 2min
B1. Box Step-up (loaded, tall bench) — 4x8 each leg — Rest 90s
B2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x10 — Rest 90s
C1. Calf Raise — 3x20 (seated + standing) — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 60s rest between rounds):
Plank 60s | Russian Twist 20 reps with 10kg | Dead Bug 15 each side

COOL-DOWN (10 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each, Quad stretch 60s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each

LONG RUN RACE SIM: ~30km / 1300m D+. Biggest session of the plan. Simulate Gypaète: active hike uphills with poles, run downhills, trot flats. Identical race nutrition (C90+salt/hour, bars, water). Full pack, mandatory gear. HR Z2 on flats, walk when exceeds 158. Test EVERYTHING: shoes, socks, anti-chafe cream.

EASY RUN (BACK-TO-BACK #2): ~10km / 300m D+ rolling terrain. Heavy legs = normal. HR strictly Z1-Z2 (<148). Don't force. Teach the body to move forward when everything says stop — this is the km 55-75 simulation for Gypaète.

Ultra Goal: Shift from max strength to muscular endurance — high reps; short rest; mimic the sustained burn of hour 8 climbs.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Glute bridges x15, Bodyweight squats x15

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Back Squat (moderate) — 3x20 — Rest 45s
A2. Walking Lunge — 3x20 each leg — Rest 45s
B1. Wall sit — 3x60s — Rest 45s
B2. Box Step-up — 3x20 each leg — Rest 45s
C1. Calf Raise — 4x20 — Rest 30s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 50s | Russian Twist 15 reps | Dead Bug 12 each side

COOL-DOWN (8 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each

Details: 10min WU + 4x8min long climbs w/ poles + 10min CD
WorkoutDescription: HILL REPEATS: 10min warm-up. Main set: 4x8min at race effort Z3 (steady HR 153-168, not explosive) WITH POLES. Recovery: technical downhill jog between reps. 10min cool-down. Grade >10%. Simulates Gypaète's sustained climbs.

Ultra Goal: Simulate one full continuous Gypaète climb (50min non-stop) — the longest vertical engine block of the prep.

WARM-UP (50 min): Stairmaster non-stop — 20min Z1 (<138), 20min Z2 (138-153), 10min Z3 (153-168)

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Row (fast) — 3x10 — Rest 60s
A2. Shoulder Press — 3x10 — Rest 60s
B1. Push-up — 3x15 — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x2, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 45s | Side Plank 30s each side

COOL-DOWN (8 min): Lat stretch 45s each, Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Calf stretch 45s each

EASY RUN: ~9km with 400m D-. Descent focus: alternate quick short steps and longer stride based on grade. Build comfort with downhill speed. Quads will burn — that's the goal. HR under 148 on climbs (walk if needed).

Ultra Goal: Prime the body for tomorrow's long dress-rehearsal run — activate without fatiguing.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x15 each, Glute bridges x15, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Back Squat (light) — 3x10 — Rest 60s
A2. Walking Lunge — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B1. Box Step-up — 3x10 each leg — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x2, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 30s | Dead Bug 10 each side

COOL-DOWN (25 min): Full mobility — Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 90s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each, Ankle mobility 60s each, Thoracic twist 45s each, Shoulder dislocates x10

LONG RUN DRESS REHEARSAL: KEY SESSION of the plan. ~37km / 1600m D+. Conditions IDENTICAL to race: early start (5-6am), full pack, poles, mandatory gear, nutrition C90+salt+bars. Target pace 10-11min/km, HR Z2 (138-153). Test 5h+ effort management. Altitude >1000m if possible. Log everything: what works, what chafes, when fatigue hits.
RECOVERY SESSION: 20min walk or easy bike (HR <138) + 25min full stretching and foam roll. Protein intake 1.8g/kg. Sleep well. Review yesterday's notes: what adjustments for race day?

Ultra Goal: Deload week — maintain upper-body pattern with 50% load while nervous system recovers.

WARM-UP (5 min): Arm circles x20, Band pull-aparts x15, Scapular push-ups x10

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Row (light 50%) — 3x10 — Rest 60s
A2. Shoulder Press (light 50%) — 3x10 — Rest 60s
B1. Push-up — 3x12 — Rest 60s
B2. Face pulls (band) — 3x15 — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x2, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 30s | Side Plank 25s each side

COOL-DOWN (10 min): Thoracic opener 60s, Lat stretch 45s each, Pec stretch 45s each
EASY RUN: ~7km / 150m D+. Active recovery. HR under 148. Everything should feel easy. If legs still heavy from S5, walk the climbs.

Ultra Goal: Pure recovery and mobility — no loading; open hips and ankles for what's ahead.

WARM-UP (25 min): Easy bike Z1 (HR <138, minimal resistance)

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Hip circles — 3x10 each side — Rest 30s
A2. Walking Lunge (deep hold 30s at bottom) — 3x6 each leg — Rest 45s
A3. Hamstring stretch (standing) — 3x45s each — Rest 15s
A4. Ankle mobility drill — 3x45s each — Rest 15s
A5. Thoracic opening (foam roller) — 3x60s — Rest 15s

CORE CIRCUIT (x2, 45s rest between rounds):
Dead Bug 10 each side | Bird dog 10 each side

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Pigeon pose 60s each, Child's pose 60s

HILL REPEATS (MAINTENANCE): 8min warm-up. Main set: 4x2min hill moderate Z3 effort (HR 153-166, not all-out). Jog down recovery. 8min cool-down. Minimum effective dose — keep hill feel without creating fatigue.
FULL REST: No exercise. Optional light stretching. Check race gear: shoes (wear?), pack, poles, flasks. Order anything missing.
LONG RUN: ~18km / 700m D+. Last significant long run. Easy Z2 pace (HR 138-153). Test FINAL gear (no new gear after this). Everything you wear today = what you wear on June 6.
FULL REST: No exercise. Morning weigh-in — should be around 94-95kg. Final 2 weeks will strip 1-2 more kg.

Ultra Goal: Final leg touch — maintain neural pattern at 40% load. No eccentric; no fatigue.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x15 each, Glute bridges x15, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Back Squat (40% reduced vs S4) — 3x10 — Rest 60s
A2. Walking Lunge — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B1. Box Step-up — 3x10 each leg — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x2, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 30s | Russian Twist 10 reps

COOL-DOWN (8 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each, Hamstring stretch 45s each
FARTLEK: 8min warm-up. Main set: 6 rounds of (1min fast pace Z3 153-168 / 2min easy Z1 <138) on rolling terrain. 8min cool-down. Feel speed in legs without accumulating fatigue. Should feel light.

Ultra Goal: Final activation — light bike; deep mobility; glute activation. Finalize race-day gear mentally during mobility.

WARM-UP (15 min): Very light bike Z1 (HR <138)

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Deep squat hold — 3x45s — Rest 30s
A2. Walking Lunge with rotation — 3x8 each leg — Rest 45s
A3. Glute Bridge (band around knees) — 3x15 — Rest 45s
A4. Clamshells (band) — 3x15 each side — Rest 30s

CORE CIRCUIT (x2, 45s rest between rounds):
Dead Bug 10 each side | Bird dog 10 each side

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Pigeon pose 60s each, Hip flexor stretch 60s each
EASY RUN + HILLS: 20min easy Z1 (<138) + 3x1min hill moderate pace Z3 (153-168). 5min cool-down + walk. Last hill session.
FULL REST: No exercise. Pack race bag: C90 (14 sachets minimum for 14h), bars, salt, flasks, poles, mandatory gear (check official list), altitude-cold clothing, headlamp.
SHAKEOUT RUN: 15min very easy jog Z1 (<138) + 4x30s progressive strides (build pace, don't sprint). 5min cool-down. Legs should feel springy, ready to run.
FULL REST: No exercise. Final weigh-in before race week. Begin carb loading: increase rice/pasta portions. Hydrate 3L.
EASY RUN: 20min very easy on flat. HR strictly under 133. Just move. Body is regenerating, each rest day makes you stronger. Glycogen loading: pasta/rice every meal.

Ultra Goal: Final pre-race activation — zero loading; pure primers. Body is loaded; do not add fatigue.

WARM-UP (10 min): Dynamic mobility — Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Arm circles x20, Scapular push-ups x10

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Glute Bridge (band) — 2x10 — Rest 30s
A2. Clamshells (band) — 2x10 each side — Rest 30s

CORE CIRCUIT (x2, 30s rest between rounds):
Plank 20s | Dead Bug 10 each side

COOL-DOWN (10 min): Pigeon pose 60s each, Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Calf stretch 45s each, Shoulder dislocates x10. Foam roll 5min.
SHAKEOUT: 10min easy Z1 (HR <133) + 3x15s progressive strides (not sprints). 3min walking cool-down. LAST run before Gypaète. Feel light and ready.

FULL REST: No exercise. MAX GLYCOGEN LOADING: 8-10g carbs/kg = 800-900g carbs today. White rice, pasta, white bread, honey, jam, juice. Hydrate 3L. No alcohol. No fiber (no raw veg/legumes). Finalize race bag.

FULL REST: No exercise. Bib pickup (check timing). Last big meal at LUNCH (pasta/rice). Dinner LIGHT and EARLY (6-7pm max): white rice + simple sauce. In bed before 9pm. Wake ~3:30am. Everything ready in the bag.

Details: Trail du Gypaète Les 3 Lacs — 76.9km / 5000 D+
WorkoutDescription: RACE DAY: Start ~5am. STRATEGY: Walk all climbs (poles, short steps, HR <158), run flats (pace 8-9min/km), technical downhills (short steps, no hard braking). Nutrition: 1 C90+salt EVERY hour + 1 bar every 2 hours + salty food at aid stations. Hydration 500-700ml/h. If low point at km 50, it's NORMAL — never quit in a trough. Target 14h.
FULL REST: No exercise. Protein + carbs within 60min post-finish. Hydrate heavily. Compression if available. No running for 7-10 days. Enjoy finishing 77km and 5000m D+ in the Aravis.
